title: Third Impact ex Editorium for peer review
display_title: Third Impact ex Editorium for peer review
wikitext:
[]: Third Impact [ex-Editorium, for peer review]             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Thu May 31, 2012 12:33 pm Hi, I have a material which I posted earlier at the general discussion forum, and I claim that this is an unambiguous large scale plot solution for NGE (which is obtained by the logical analysis). The material I posted earlier contains some inaccuracies and minor logical errors, but now it's logically polished and I want to make available it or a link to it on the wiki. The analysis is based on a preposition that there are no major unambiguities in the underlying NGE plot, and I not found evidences that this preposition does not hold. The reason I want to make it available on the wiki is that it completely destroys some theories placed here, and it very hard to convince people in opposite, because they consider the material placed at the wiki as absolutely credible. I don't insist that the rest part of wiki should be edited according to the analysis, but the part which gives the most of troubles is the theory about that angels should return to Adam and this will generate the Third Impact. This is definitely a lie (so Seele actually have absolutely no intentions to protect the humanity from angels because there is no need in this, but they want to protect Lilith from angels) and it confuses all the people who do not want to analyze but just to read theories, and there should be at least note about that "it could be a lie inspired by Seele". Also I don't insist that the analysis should be considered as the ultimate truth, it's intended mostly for the people which are able to analyze and operate with facts. Also, I need guides where I can place it, if it's possible. Thanks. The material is available here .

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu May 31, 2012 1:08 pm We don't really have a place for posting overall analysis of the show, a lot of that gets churned through the forum since you usually won't get a large consensus on analysis or interpretations on everything that makes up a generalized approach. We're more likely to get wider consensus on specific points, which is why the wiki focuses on specifics (See the Theory and Analysis section). I think you have a thread on your analysis somewhere, if you want to repost it in that thread for discussion, that's probably your best bet. Most everything in that section of the wiki has gone through years of discussion and analysis (most of them will link to relevant threads here or in the thread archive), and sometimes continues to get updated.

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Thu May 31, 2012 1:12 pm [quote=" Ornette"][/quote] OK, but I think that it's critical to add a remark on the section about the Third Impact generated by angels. Please see the analysis, if you need a base.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 02, 2012 12:32 pm gchristnsn wrote: OK, but I think that it's critical to add a remark on the section about the Third Impact generated by angels. Please see the analysis, if you need a base.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jun 02, 2012 2:27 pm Well, the section could use an update, the content for that section was the one that V originally put up there, with later rewordings to remove the POV inaccuracy. It probably wouldn't be a bad idea to note that an Angel coming in contact with Adam could be faux, as it doesn't conflict with external sources like the theatrical programs and the CI (note that they all use pillow words like "could be", "is believed to be", etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jun 02, 2012 9:48 pm Ornette wrote: Well, the section could use an update, the content for that section was the one that V originally put up there, with later rewordings to remove the POV inaccuracy. It probably wouldn't be a bad idea to note that an Angel coming in contact with Adam could be faux, as it doesn't conflict with external sources like the theatrical programs and the CI (note that they all use pillow words like "could be", "is believed to be", etc.

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sun Jun 03, 2012 2:30 am Bagheera wrote: I don't see a need for that. You assume that Kaworu was deceived, that SEELE were lying about an Angel-generated 3I, and that no such event would occur should an Angel come into contact with Adam (much less Lilith), but we don't know any of that for certain. Because of that I think the change you're advocating is premature; the blurb on that page is cautious enough that it's not asserting anything as a given, and the inferences it makes are reasonable. But since that's off-topic for this thread it would probably be best to make a separate topic on the subject (in this forum) if you want to discuss it further.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jun 03, 2012 10:15 am gchristnsn wrote: Because angels are already complete beings who have no any idea of society

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sun Jun 03, 2012 11:26 am Bagheera wrote: No, that's not true. Leliel, Arael, Armisael, and Kaworu show us beyond all doubt that Angels are human beings who are as lost and lonely as any of us. They desire complementation just like we do. This is not a deception by SEELE -- it's what we see in the very scenes in which these beings appear. Also, pay close attention to Misato's monologues in EoE. She lays out the Angels' nature, and the tragedy of their conflict with humanity, pretty clearly.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jun 03, 2012 7:03 pm gchristnsn wrote: I know, it's look too bold, but not for people who could reason. Sorry, if I insulted you.

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Mon Jun 04, 2012 2:02 am Bagheera wrote: That doesn't come off as very sincere given that you're doing it again right here. I'm perfectly capable of reasoning, thanks. I just don't find your argument compelling since it seems to me that you're cherry picking your facts and ignoring those that don't suit your conclusions. But in any event I don't think we're going to get anywhere here. If others care to weigh in on the subject we can try pursuing it further then.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Jun 04, 2012 2:41 am I think we've reached the point where everyone needs to chill.

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Mon Jun 04, 2012 6:28 am [quote=" Mr. Tines":174umo0r][/quote:174umo0r] There actually is nothing more to discuss, you may consider my opinion as credible or not as an arbiter, and the only thing I want is to add such a remark at the wiki. I don't ask rights as an editor (and I think this is not necessary), so this may be not me.

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Mon Jun 04, 2012 10:15 am gchristnsn wrote: ... Armisael doesn't know what solitude is, and this is no problem for them, ...

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Mon Jun 04, 2012 1:44 pm thewayneiac wrote: I can easily show that this isn't true. Rei makes Armisael realize that the emotion she's been feeling is loneliness. Armisael responds by trying to resurrect her fellow Angels through the Angel Tower.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 04, 2012 5:03 pm gchristnsn wrote: My opinion is that Amisrael makes Rei to realize that she is full of grief and solitude (there is a common pattern - angels are psychoanalysts for the pilots). This is necessary to make Rei to fuse with Shinji's unit (here again is the cause for the motivation).

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Tue Jun 05, 2012 2:07 am Reichu wrote: It's not either-or here. Armisael says, "I give you this pain. It hurts, doesn't it?" Rei tells Armi that the "pain" is actually loneliness and explains what that means. Since the two individuals are connected physically and mentally, Armisael is able to use this new knowledge to tell Rei that she, too, is alone, a feeling Rei has been denying she's had even though she's known all along what it is. I don't know where you get the impression that Rei is "made to fuse" with Eva-01. Rei acknowledges that the action represents her innermost feelings, but it's clear that the fusion is the Angel's own doing and not something Rei can control in any meaningful way... considering she stops the process by blowing herself and Armisael up, as opposed to stopping the actual fusion.

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sat Jun 09, 2012 6:43 am A yet another update (it's loosely related to the topic, but if someone is interested): There is a set of manipulations which begins with Yui, so Yui probably had chosen Gendo as an appropriate candidate for the ark scenario (there was obviously no true love between Yui and Gendo but not vice-versa), and she probably hoped that he will inflict injuries to others (as he actually did, he manipulated by Naoko (which had resulted in suicide), Ritsuko, Rei, Shinji, etc. And this set of manipulations in the absence of the true love is probably the "tragedy of people possessed by Eva", as Ritsuko said. Yui probably intended to ruin ark scenario (as it actually happened, and Yui got her punishment for this - eternal solitude, death was Gendo's punishment) by using Gendo's personality, but this could be only hope and the scenario itself is a fail-safe plan against Seele's complementation (there is principal difference between the plans, Seele wished to unify humanity through death as it was prescribed in the scrolls (a message of the creators) to not get a being which is equal to the creators, the ark scenario implies creation of a being which is equal to the creators of the humanity through direct complementation).

[]:             Quote Post by thewayneiac » Sat Jun 09, 2012 10:30 am gchristnsn wrote: (there was obviously no true love between Yui and Gendo but not vice-versa),

[]:             Quote Post by gchristnsn » Sat Jun 09, 2012 11:04 am thewayneiac wrote: Why do you say that? She seems to be happy with him.

The material I posted earlier contains some inaccuracies and minor logical errors, but now it's logically polished and I
